name: Tequila Chicken with Tomatillo Sauce
recipeIngredient: Chicken breast halves with bone and skin
recipeInstructions:
1. Wear rubber gloves when slicing and seeding peppers.
2. Mix marinade ingredients in a plastic food bag. Add chicken and close the bag, squeezing out as much air as possible. Marinate in the refrigerator 4-8 hours, turning the bag once.
3. For the tomatillo sauce: Put tomatillos, jalapenos, tequila, and all but 2 tablespoons of chicken broth into a medium-sized saucepan. Cover and cook over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes until tomatillos are tender. Stir in flour and remaining 2 tablespoons of chicken broth until smooth. Stir this mixture into the tomatillo mixture. Add cilantro and salt. Simmer for 4-5 minutes over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is slightly thickened.
4. Puree the sauce in a blender or food processor.
5. Remove chicken from the marinade. Discard the marinade.
6. To grill: Cook chicken 4-6 inches above hot coals for about 15-20 minutes, turning once, until opaque in the center when pierced with the tip of a knife.
7. To broil: Broil 4-5 inches from heat source as directed above.
8. Pour 1 cup hot tomatillo sauce onto a serving platter. Arrange chicken on top of the sauce. Serve sauce separately.
Per Serving (with 1/3 cup sauce): 150 calories, 18 g protein, 4 g carbohydrates, 6 g fat, 46 mg cholesterol, 438 mg sodium. Source: Magazine clipping.
Recipe by JUDI M. PHELPS (BNVX05A).
